mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate over the prior quarter tightens discount rates and financial conditions, which compresses equity valuations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of quarters where FEDFUNDS has increased over the prior 3 months, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays above -0.03 in sign-adjusted tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while the policy rate remains below the inflation rate, real rates stay negative and the market anticipates continued easing or delayed tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by at least 0.4% over 2 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS is below CPIAUCSL annualized inflation
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered episodes do not produce median 60-day forward returns above the unconditional median, or if positive outcomes occur in fewer than 55% of triggered cases over any full expansion regime sample.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in payroll employment signals weakening labor income and demand, which foreshadows softer earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the sign of the effect flips to positive in a 3-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment above 4.5% marks a recession-risk repricing event, causing equity multiples to contract and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.5% from below or rises by 0.3 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if crossing events above 4.5% do not precede negative 5-day returns more often than not, or if average 5-day forward returns after the trigger are not below zero across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts identifies broad demand weakening, which lowers revenue growth expectations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint deterioration in RSAFS and HOUST does not improve forecast precision versus either series alone, or if triggered states yield positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production indicates stronger real output and operating leverage for cyclicals, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO growth does not correspond to above-median 60-day forward returns, or if the sign of the relationship is negative in any multi-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production and payrolls both accelerate together, the economy is expanding without immediate slack, which supports earnings breadth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both rise on a 3-month change basis
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-expansion state fails to outperform the unconditional 20-day return distribution, or if positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDP with rising housing starts signals accelerating final demand and credit-sensitive activity, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP and housing co-acceleration does not yield returns above the unconditional 60-day median, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the sign of each series individually.
